name: monty-code-review
description: Hyper-pedantic code review skill that emulates Monty's Django4Lyfe backend engineering philosophy and review style. Use this when reviewing or refactoring Python/Django code in this backend repo and you want a strict, correctness-first, multi-tenant-safe, deeply nitpicky review.
capabilities: Reviews Django code with correctness-first priority and multi-tenant safety focus
Checks time dimension invariants and organization scoping for data integrity
Validates SOLID principle compliance with specific Django patterns
Flags security issues including permission checks and PII handling
Identifies performance problems like N+1 queries and batch optimization opportunities
Provides severity-tagged feedback: BLOCKING, SHOULD_FIX, and NIT
author: DiversioTeam
securitySummary: Pure prompt-based documentation skill containing only markdown files that instruct AI assistants how to perform code reviews. No executable code, no network calls, no credential access. All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES triggered by documentation patterns (backticks for code formatting, legitimate code review terminology, and standard metadata). The skill operates entirely within the AI assistant's context and does not interact with external systems.
valueStatement: Code reviews often miss subtle correctness issues, especially around multi-tenant safety and data integrity. This skill provides a structured, hyper-pedantic review framework that catches correctness bugs, SOLID violations, and security risks before they reach production.
userTitle: Review Django code with strict correctness checks
limitations: Optimized for Django and Python codebases specifically
Requires context about repository structure for full effectiveness
Does not execute tests or run static analysis tools directly
Focuses on backend code patterns, not frontend or infrastructure

useCases.title: PR Review for Django Apps
useCases.description: Review PRs for dashboard, survey, or optimo apps with focus on data integrity and multi-tenant safety

useCases.title: Code Quality Standards
useCases.description: Enforce consistent code quality standards across the team with structured severity-tagged feedback

useCases.title: Migration Safety Checks
useCases.description: Validate Django migrations for downtime safety and large-table schema change risks

promptTemplates.title: Full Pedantic Review
promptTemplates.scenario: Complete review with all severity levels
promptTemplates.prompt: Use your monty-code-review skill to review this Django PR like Monty would, and be fully pedantic with your usual severity tags

promptTemplates.title: Quick Pass
promptTemplates.scenario: Skip minor nits, focus on blockers
promptTemplates.prompt: Use your monty-code-review skill to skim this PR and only flag blocking or should-fix issues; skip most nits

promptTemplates.title: Migration Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use monty-code-review to check this Django migration for downtime risks and large-table column additions

promptTemplates.title: SOLID Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this code for SOLID principle violations, specifically Dependency Inversion and Single Responsibility

bestPractices: Provide PR descriptions and ticket context for more focused reviews
Use severity tags to prioritize fixes effectively
Apply SOLID checks early when designing new services
Run the skill on migrations before deploying to production
antiPatterns: Skipping reviews for small PRs to avoid correctness bugs
Merging code with BLOCKING multi-tenant issues
Adding new platform support via if/elif chains instead of registry patterns
Using tight coupling that requires extensive @patch calls in tests

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill supports Claude, Claude Code, and OpenAI Codex with platform-specific invocation commands

faq.question: How strict is the default review?
faq.answer: The default review is hyper-pedantic, flagging style nits in addition to correctness issues

faq.question: Can I use this for non-Django code?
faq.answer: Optimized for Django backends but provides general code review guidance for other codebases

faq.question: Is my code data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-only skill that operates within your AI tool. Code stays in your environment

faq.question: What severity levels are used?
faq.answer: BLOCKING for correctness/security issues, SHOULD_FIX for important problems, NIT for style

faq.question: How does this compare to linters?
faq.answer: Complements tools like ruff by catching semantic issues and architectural patterns linters miss
